item.title: About me
item.description: Name My name is 杨 海平 (Yang Haiping), where the firstname is from a Chinese poem 春江花月夜. The first character 海 means ‘sea/ocean’, and the second character 平 means ‘calm/flat’. Research I am interested (geometric) representation theory in general. More specifically, I am interested in the theory of D-modules and its applications in representation theory. My research statement can be found here (last updated 11/2021). Life Story I was born in Heilongjiang, China, and lived there until 2009.

item.title: Papers and Notes
item.description: Papers and Preprints [Hochschild–de Rham Homology] This will be my second paper, which is on a generalisation of Poisson–de Rham Homology of Etingof–Schedler. Coming up soon… On the Derived Ring of Differential Operators on a Singularity My first paper, which is on the cateogry of D-modules on singular varieties viewed as DG-modules over a DG-algebra. Notes Symplectic Representation Theory

item.title: Short CV
item.description: Education Imperial College London PhD in Mathematics 2018-2022 Supervisor: Travis Schedler Research Area: Representation Theory Industrial Strategy EPSRC Scholarship University of Southern California PhD in Mathematics 2017-2018 (unfinished) Imperial College London MSci in Mathematics First Class Honour 2013-2017 Rector’s Undergraduate Scholarship Master Project: Modular Representation Theory of Finite Groups (with Alexander Ivanov) Seminars Orgainsed
